🇨🇦🇺🇸Step-by-step guide

The Complete Guide to US Credit Cards for Canadians

Everything you need to know about getting an ITIN, building US credit, and accessing the world's best rewards cards

25 min read•Last updated February 2026
🧭

Try the Interactive Step-by-Step Guide

Follow along one step at a time with checklists, progress tracking, and your place saved automatically.

Start Guide

In this guide

Why US Cards?1. US Address2. Bank Account3. First Card4. Get ITIN5. Build Credit6. Chase Cards7. ExpandManaging CardsMistakes to AvoidFirst-Year Roadmap

Why Should Canadians Get US Credit Cards?

If you're into travel rewards, the US credit card market is an entirely different league. We're talking bigger sign-up bonuses, more transfer partners, and perks that Canadian issuers can only dream of offering. Here's the quick pitch:

Bigger Welcome Bonuses

US Amex Platinum offers 150k+ MR points. Canadian Platinum? Usually 70-80k.

More Transfer Partners

Amex US has 21 airline transfer partners vs just 11 in Canada. That's nearly double the options for finding sweet-spot redemptions.

Elite Status from Cards

Hold the Hilton Aspire and you get Diamond status. Marriott Bonvoy Brilliant gives you Platinum. No stays required.

No FX Fees

Most premium US cards waive foreign transaction fees, so you can use them for Canadian spending without the 2.5% surcharge.

The catch? It takes some upfront work — getting a US address, building credit history from scratch, and navigating the ITIN process. But thousands of Canadians have done it, and the payoff is absolutely worth it. Let's walk through it step by step.

The Step-by-Step Process

1

Before anything else, you need a US residential address. This is where your cards, bank statements, and IRS correspondence will be mailed. You have two options:

Option A: Mail forwarding service. Companies like 24/7 Parcel, US Global Mail, and Traveling Mailbox give you a real street address (not a PO box) and scan or forward your mail. Expect to pay $70–90 USD per year.

Option B: Friend or family in the US. If you have someone willing to receive mail for you, this is the cheapest option. Just make sure they're okay with occasional IRS letters and credit card mail.

Critical check: Go to the USPS address lookup tool and verify your address. Look for "Commercial Mail Receiving Agency" — it must show "N". If it shows "Y", card issuers like Chase may flag and reject your applications. Some mail forwarding services are flagged as commercial; always verify before signing up.
Pro tip: Check your address at USPS.com before committing to any service. Search your address in their zip code lookup tool — if the result includes "Commercial Mail Receiving Agency: Y", find a different provider.
2

You need a US bank account for two things: paying your credit card bills in USD, and having a bank statement with a US address for card issuer verification.

The easiest path is through Canadian banks with US subsidiaries:

  • CIBC US (formerly Simplii Financial US) — The most popular choice. No monthly fee on the Smart Account, and you can open it online from Canada.
  • TD Bank — Great if you're already a TD Canada customer. TD has branches across the US East Coast.
  • BMO Harris — BMO's US banking arm. Easy cross-border setup.
  • RBC Bank — RBC's US presence, primarily in the Southeast.
Make sure to set your US mailing address as the primary address on this account. You'll use bank statements from this account as proof of address when applying for credit cards.
3

This is where the magic happens. Amex Global Transfer lets you leverage your existing Canadian Amex relationship to get approved for a US Amex card — no US credit history or ITIN required.

Requirements:

  • An existing Canadian Amex card, open for at least 3 months
  • Your US mailing address
  • Your US bank account

How to apply:

  • Online: Apply on the US Amex website. During the application, check the box that says you have an existing Amex card from another country. Enter your Canadian Amex card details.
  • Phone: Call the Amex Global Transfer line and they'll walk you through it.

You may need to upload your passport and a bank statement showing your US address for verification. This is normal — just have them ready.

Start with a personal card, not a business card. Business cards do not build personal credit history in the US. Your first card needs to be a personal card so it starts building your credit file with the bureaus.

Best starter cards:

Amex Hilton Honors— no annual fee — great keeperAmex Gold Card— strong earning
Choose wisely: Your first US card will be your oldest account on your US credit report forever. Pick something you'll want to keep long-term. A no-annual-fee card like the Hilton Honors is a popular choice for exactly this reason.
4

An Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) is essentially a tax ID number issued by the IRS for people who aren't eligible for a Social Security Number. You need this to apply for cards from Chase, Citi, Capital One, Bank of America, and most non-Amex issuers.

Method 1: Use a Tax Service (Easiest)

Services like US Tax Resources and FrugalFlyer's ITIN service handle the entire process for $150–300. They prepare your W-7 form and file a 1040-NR tax return on your behalf. This is the hands-off option if you don't want to deal with IRS forms.

Method 2: DIY with the Gambling Income Method (~$10)

The DIY method is straightforward and costs under $10. You file a 1040-NR tax return declaring a small amount of US-source gambling income ($75–100 USD) from US-based online casinos. You self-declare the income — no proof of gambling is required. This creates a valid reason to need an ITIN and you'll owe about $9 USD in taxes.

You fill out Form W-7 (the ITIN application), Form 1040-NR (non-resident tax return), Schedule 1, and Schedule OI. You'll also need your Canadian SIN and a list of dates you were physically present in the US during the tax year.

How to Submit Your Application

Recommended
Path A: In-Person at IRS Taxpayer Assistance Center
  • ✅ Best option — bring your passport, no mailing needed, get it stamped as received
  • 📞 Call 844-545-5640 to book appointment (up to 2 months ahead)
  • 📞 Backup: 267-941-1000 (not toll-free, works from Canadian numbers)
  • 📋 Bring: completed W-7, 1040-NR, Schedule 1, Schedule OI, passport, duplicate copies of all forms
  • ⏱️ Appointment takes 20 min to 1 hour
  • ℹ️ The clerk checks completeness only — they don't approve/deny. Remind them you're filing a 1040-NR.
Path B: Mail-In
  • 📬 Mail to: Internal Revenue Service, ITIN Operation, P.O. Box 149342, Austin, TX 78714-9342, USA
  • 📮 Send via Canada Post registered mail (~$15) for tracking/proof
  • 🛂 You must include your original passport — IRS no longer accepts certified copies
Your passport can take ~5 months to return. Some people have had passports lost in transit. Do you have upcoming travel that needs your passport? If so, choose Path A.

📝 Line-by-Line Form Instructions

Follow along field by field — most fields are left blank

Don't be intimidated! The whole process looks scarier than it is. Most fields are left blank. The key entries are your name, address, SIN, passport info, and a small gambling income number.

⚠️ Form line numbers may change year to year. Always verify against the current year's forms from irs.gov.

1Form W-7 — ITIN Application

Top right checkbox: Check "Apply for a New ITIN"

Reason for applying: Check box (b) — filing a tax return

Name section: Must match your passport exactly

Mailing address: Your Canadian address is fine — the IRS returns documents here

Foreign address: Write your address again even if same as mailing address

Birth information: Complete as on passport. If your birth country is not recognized by the USA, use a recognized country.

6a — Countries of citizenship: Enter all countries you hold citizenship in

6b — Foreign Tax ID Number: Enter your Canadian SIN — this is REQUIRED

Type of US Visa: Leave blank (unless you have a work visa — if so, get professional help)

Identification Document: Check "Passport", enter Canada, your passport number, and expiry date. Leave "date of entry" blank.

6e: No  |  6f, 6g: Leave blank

Sign Here: Sign in ink (NOT digital), enter date and phone number (Canadian number is fine)

Acceptance Agent section: Do not complete

2Form 1040-NR — Non-Resident Tax Return

Filing status: Single (or Married Filing Separately / Qualifying Widower)

Name: Must match your W-7 exactly

Identifying number: Leave blank — you're applying for one!

Address: Your Canadian mailing address

Virtual currency question: Just put Yes or No (about crypto — you don't need to report income)

Dependents: Leave blank

💰 Income & Tax Lines (using $85 gambling income as example):

Line 8$85
Line 9$85
Line 11$85
Line 15$85
Line 16$9
Line 18$9
Line 22$9
Line 24$9
Line 26$0
Line 32$0
Line 33$0
Line 37$9

Line 16 ($9) comes from the IRS Tax Table for income $75–$99. Pick any amount in that range (e.g. $85).

Line 38: Leave blank (income under $1,000 = no penalty)

Third Party Designee: Check "No"

Sign Here: Sign in ink, enter date and occupation. Phone/email optional. Leave PIN blank.

Paid Preparer: Leave blank (unless using a paid service — they MUST include their PTIN)

3Schedule OI — Other Information

Name: Your name at the top of the page

A: Country of citizenship during tax year

B: Canada (if you live/work in Canada)

C, D: Expected to be "No"

E: Write "Not present in U.S.—No U.S. immigration status" (unless you were in the US on Dec 31)

F: No

G: List all dates you entered/left the USA that year (including layovers). Check travel records at i94.cbp.dhs.gov. Leave blank if you weren't in the US.

H: Number of days present in USA for each of the 3 tax years.

Substantial Presence Test: If (current year days × 1) + (prior year × ⅓) + (2 years ago × ⅙) ≥ 183, you may be treated as a US tax resident. Get professional help immediately.

I, J, K: Expected "No"

L, M: Leave blank

4Schedule 1 — Additional Income

Line 8b: Enter your gambling income amount (same as 1040-NR Line 8, e.g. $85)

Everything else: Leave blank or $0

Payment (~$9 USD)

  • 💵 Pay by check (payable to "United States Treasury") included with your mailing, or pay online at irs.gov/payments
  • 📋 Late filing penalty caps at 100% of tax (~$9 max penalty), plus a few dollars interest
  • 😌 Don't stress about being "late" — the total additional cost is minimal

After Submission

  • ⏱️ Processing: 6–8 weeks
  • 📬 You'll receive a letter with your 9-digit ITIN
  • 🚨 IMMEDIATELY link it to your Amex US account
  • 🔄 ITIN expires if not used on a tax return every 3 years — file periodically to keep it active
  • 📞 Lost your letter? Call +1 267-941-1000 with your name, address, and birthdate to retrieve it
You don't need to wait for your ITIN to start building credit — that's why we get the Amex card first via Global Transfer. Apply for your ITIN in parallel while your Amex card is building your credit history.

⚖️ Disclaimer: This guide is for informational purposes only and does not constitute tax advice. Consult a tax professional for your specific situation.

5

Now you play the waiting game. Your first Amex card is reporting to the US credit bureaus, and you need to build up enough history before other issuers will approve you.

  • Get 1-2 more Amex personal cards in your first 3-6 months. Each new personal card adds another account to your credit file.
  • Amex business cards don't count for Chase's 5/24 rule — feel free to grab Amex Business Platinum, Business Gold, etc. during this period. They won't hurt your future Chase applications.
  • Keep utilization at 5-10% on all your cards. Low utilization signals responsible credit use.
  • Open a Chase checking account at a US branch if you can visit. Having a banking relationship with Chase significantly helps approval odds later.
Critical: Once you receive your ITIN, call Amex immediately and have them link it to all your existing US accounts. This ensures your credit history is properly tied to your ITIN in the bureau files. Without this step, Chase and other issuers may not be able to pull your credit.
6

Chase is the holy grail for most churners — their Ultimate Rewards points are incredibly flexible, and their co-branded hotel cards are best-in-class. But Chase is also the pickiest issuer.

What Chase wants:

  • At least 12-18 months of US credit history
  • A credit score above ~700 (check on Credit Karma US)
  • Ideally, a Chase banking relationship

The 5/24 Rule

Chase will automatically deny you if you've opened 5 or more personal credit cards (across all issuers) in the past 24 months. This is why we're strategic about getting Amex business cards first — they don't count toward 5/24.

Recommended first Chase cards:

Chase Sapphire Preferred— great starterInk Business Preferred— doesn't count for 5/24Chase Aeroplan Card— useful for Canadians
If you can visit a US Chase branch, apply in-person. Chase sometimes requires document verification (passport, bank statements) for applicants without a Social Security Number. Being in-branch makes this much smoother.
7

Once you've got Chase cards and solid credit history, the world opens up:

  • Capital One: Typically wants ~3 years of credit history. They have a strict maximum of 1 application per 6 months. Worth it for the Venture X.
  • Citi: The Strata Premier (formerly Premier) is excellent. Citi ThankYou points transfer to a great set of airlines including Air Canada Aeroplan.
  • Bank of America: The Alaska Airlines card is a fan favourite. Apply in-branch or by phone if using an ITIN — their online system doesn't always handle ITINs well.

Keep building and maintaining your credit. Pay all balances in full every month, keep accounts open, and space out applications. A mature US credit profile unlocks better approval odds and higher credit limits over time.

Managing Your US Cards from Canada

Paying Your Bills

Don't use your bank's exchange rate — you'll lose 2-2.5% on every transfer. Instead, use dedicated FX services like Wise, VBCE, or Knightsbridge FX. They typically charge ~1% above the spot rate, saving you significant money over time.

Using US Cards in Canada

Here's a nice perk: most US premium cards have no foreign transaction fees. That means you can use them for everyday Canadian spending and earn US points without the 2.5% FX surcharge you'd pay on Canadian cards abroad. Just be mindful of the CAD→USD→CAD conversion.

Two-Player Mode

If you have a spouse or partner, set them up simultaneously. Two people means double the welcome bonuses, double the points, and the ability to share points between accounts. It's the single biggest force multiplier in the churning game.

Common Mistakes to Avoid

Getting too many cards too fast. If you rack up 5+ personal cards in 24 months before applying to Chase, you're locked out. Plan your sequence.

Not linking your ITIN to existing Amex accounts. Without this, your credit history may not be visible to other issuers. Call Amex as soon as you receive your ITIN.

Using bank FX rates to pay bills. The 2-2.5% spread adds up fast. Use Wise, VBCE, or Knightsbridge FX instead.

Cancelling your oldest US card. Your first US card is the anchor of your credit history. Downgrade it to a no-fee version if needed, but never close it.

Not checking if your mail address is flagged as commercial. Chase and other issuers can and will reject applications if your address shows up as a "Commercial Mail Receiving Agency" on USPS.

Starting with a business card. Business cards don't report to personal credit bureaus. Your first card must be a personal card to start building your credit file.

Recommended First-Year Card Sequence

Here's a realistic timeline for your first 18+ months of US credit cards. Every situation is different, but this is a solid starting framework:

Month 0

Amex Hilton Honors (No-Fee Keeper)

Your anchor card. No annual fee, so you'll keep it forever as your oldest account.

Month 1-2

Apply for ITIN

Submit your W-7 via a tax service or DIY. Processing takes ~8 weeks.

Month 3

Amex Hilton Aspire or Amex Gold

Second personal card. Builds credit file depth. Choose based on current offers.

Month 3-6

Amex Business Platinum + Business Gold

Business cards don't count toward 5/24. Grab these freely for the bonuses.

Month 4

Receive ITIN → Link to All Accounts

Call Amex immediately to link your ITIN to every US account you have.

Month 12-18

Chase Sapphire Preferred + Ink Business Preferred

You now have enough credit history for Chase. Start with their best cards.

Month 18+

Chase United, IHG, Hyatt → Capital One, Citi

Continue expanding your portfolio. Space applications 3+ months apart.

Ready to Start?

Browse our US cards database to find the best current welcome bonuses and start planning your sequence.
